MICOM - wastewateR Worksheet Answer Key

Author

Julie (Jules) Gilbert

Getting Started

Introduction to Laboratory Data Format

# set the location of the data files
data_file_loc <- "C:/Users/juliegil/University of Michigan Dropbox/SPH-MICOM/CORES/ModelingAndAnalytics/misc/insightnet2026_demo/data_files/"

one_file <- read.csv(paste0(data_file_loc, "03.25.26_GT_fiveplex1_QX600.csv"))
# view the column names of this file
colnames(one_file)
 [1] "Well"                            "Sample.description.1"           
 [3] "Sample.description.2"            "Sample.description.3"           
 [5] "Sample.description.4"            "Target"                         
 [7] "Conc.copies.µL."                 "pg.µL"

[57] "TotalCNVMax68"                   "TotalCNVMin68"                  
[59] "PoissonCNVMax68"                 "PoissonCNVMin68"                
[61] "TotalRatioMax68"                 "TotalRatioMin68"                
[63] "PoissonRatioMax68"               "PoissonRatioMin68"              
[65] "TotalFractionalAbundanceMax68"   "TotalFractionalAbundanceMin68"  
[67] "PoissonFractionalAbundanceMax68" "PoissonFractionalAbundanceMin68"

# view the sample names 
table(one_file$Sample.description.1)

23MM260312I 23MM260314I 23MM260315I 26BB260317I 26BB260322I 26CC260317I 
         12          12          12          12          12          12 
26CC260322I 26HH260316I 26HH260322I 26JJ260317I 26JJ260322I 26LL260318I 
         12          12          12          12          12          12 
26LL260323I         NEG         NTC         POS 
         12          12          12          12 
# view the pathogen targets that were tested for
table(one_file$Target)

FluA FluB  RSV  SC2 
  48   48   48   48 
one_file <- one_file %>% mutate(rows = substr(Well, 1, 1), 
                                columns = substr(Well, 2, 3))


ggplot() + 
  geom_tile(data = one_file, aes(x = columns, y = rows, fill = Positives)) + 
  geom_text(data = one_file, aes(x = columns, y = rows, label = Positives)) 
ggplot() + 
  geom_tile(data = one_file, aes(x = columns, y = rows, fill = Positives)) + 
  geom_text(data = one_file, aes(x = columns, y = rows, label = Positives)) + 
  scale_y_discrete(limits=rev) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(x = "", 
       y = "") + 
  facet_wrap(.~Target)

Processing Laboratory Data for QAQC

# get a vector of our lab file names
lab_files <- list.files(path = data_file_loc, pattern = "*.csv")

# read in the first file in our vector
file_in <- read.csv(paste0(data_file_loc, lab_files[1]))

# use the software_renaming function 
file_in2 <- software_renaming(file_in)
# use our qaqc wrapper
output1 <- qaqc_processing_fiveplex(file_in = file_in2, 
                         lab_site_ids = c("26BB", "26CC", "26HH", 
                                          "26JJ", "23MM", "26LL", 
                                          "23ZZ"))

End wrapper for FivePlex.
returned_sample_data <- output1[1][[1]]

returned_error_data <- output1[2][[1]]

Extra data prep code, but pre-made file is available for use:

all_data <- data.frame()

for (file_number in seq(1, length(lab_files))){
  
    # read in the first file in our vector
    file_in <- read.csv(paste0(data_file_loc, lab_files[file_number]))

    # use the software_renaming function 
    file_in2 <- software_renaming(file_in)
    
    # use our qaqc wrapper
    output1 <- suppressMessages(qaqc_processing_fiveplex(file_in = file_in2, 
                         lab_site_ids = c("26BB", "26CC", "26HH", 
                                          "26JJ", "23MM", "26LL", 
                                          "23ZZ")))
    
    returned_sample_data <- output1[1][[1]]
    
    returned_sample_data$plate_name <- lab_files[file_number]
    
    all_data <- rbind(all_data, returned_sample_data)

}

Calculations and Data Display - Gene Copies per 100mL

comp_data_loc <- "C:/Users/juliegil/University of Michigan Dropbox/SPH-MICOM/CORES/ModelingAndAnalytics/misc/insightnet2026_demo/compiled_data/"

all_data <- read.csv(paste0(comp_data_loc, "post_process_compiled.csv"))
pre_lab_measurements <- read.csv(paste0(comp_data_loc, "pre_processing_data.csv"))
all_data <- all_data %>% mutate(CP_uL = CopiesPer20uLWell / 5)

gc_calculations <- calculate_gc_per_100ml(lab_df_in = all_data,
                                          all_weigh_info = pre_lab_measurements)
There are 11 sample names that were in the laboratory data set that were not in the weight info dataset.
The samples that have been lost due to merging inconsistencies are: 
NEG
POS
NTC
23ZZ260404I
23ZZ260401I
23ZZ260402I
23ZZ260403I
23ZZ260405I
23ZZ260406I
23ZZ260408I
23ZZ260410I
# merge triplicate samples into 1 value

gc_calc_one_value <- gc_calculations %>% group_by(plate_name, Sample, Target) %>% summarize(mean_limit_of_detection = mean(detection_limit_CP_100mL, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                                                                            mean_gc_per_100mL = mean(CP_100_mL_of_sample, na.rm = TRUE))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'plate_name', 'Sample'. You can override
using the `.groups` argument.
# logic for above/below lod

gc_calc_one_value <- gc_calc_one_value %>% mutate(relative_lod = case_when(mean_gc_per_100mL > mean_limit_of_detection ~ "above", 
                                                                        T ~ "below"))

# get sample collection date from sample name
gc_calc_one_value <- gc_calc_one_value %>% mutate(sample_collection_date = as.POSIXct(substr(Sample, 5, 10), format = "%y%m%d"), 
                                                  collection_site = substr(Sample, 1, 4))
# make a chart

ggplot() + 
  geom_line(data = gc_calc_one_value, aes(x = sample_collection_date, y = mean_gc_per_100mL, color = collection_site)) + 
  geom_point(data = gc_calc_one_value, aes(x = sample_collection_date, y = mean_gc_per_100mL, shape = relative_lod, color = collection_site)) + 
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("above" = 16, "below" = 1)) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(x = "Sample Collection Date", 
       y = "Gene Copies per 100mL", 
       shape = "Limit of Detection", 
       color = "Collection Site", 
       title = "One Month of Respiratory Disease Wastewater Testing") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1)) + 
  facet_wrap(.~Target)

Bonus Section! Displaying Data as Concentration per uL

# read in our data
all_data <- read.csv(paste0(comp_data_loc, "post_process_compiled.csv"))

# select the negative control well data
neg_controls <- filter(all_data, grepl("NEG", Sample))

# keep only the necessary columns for use in calculate_lod_invgamm()
neg_controls <- neg_controls %>% select(Sample, Target, plate_name, Positives, AcceptedDroplets)

# create a plate_date column that corresponds to when the plate was run/generated
neg_controls <- neg_controls %>% mutate(plate_date = as.POSIXct(substr(x = plate_name, start = 1, stop = 8), format = "%m.%d.%y"))

# reorder the columns
neg_controls <- neg_controls %>% select(Sample, Target, plate_name, plate_date, Positives, AcceptedDroplets)

# rename the columns
colnames(neg_controls) <- c("Sample", "Target", "plate", "plate_date", "Positives", "AcceptedDroplets")

lod_df <- calculate_lod_invgamm(neg_controls)
# merge sample data from multiples to 1 value

merged_all <- merging_methods(all_data, method = "average")

# create bridge table

bridge_table <- all_data %>% select(Sample, Target, plate_name) %>% distinct()

# join lod_df onto this merged dataset
step_one <- merge(merged_all, bridge_table, by = c("Sample", "Target"))

final_conc_set <- merge(step_one, lod_df, by.x = c("plate_name", "Target"), by.y = c("plate", "Target"))

# make indicator column
final_conc_set <- final_conc_set %>% mutate(relative_lod = case_when(Concentration > NEG_limit_final ~ "above", 
                                                         T ~ "below"))

# get site and date
final_conc_set <- final_conc_set %>% 
mutate(sample_collection_date = as.POSIXct(substr(Sample, 5, 10), format = "%y%m%d"), 
       collection_site = substr(Sample, 1, 4))
# make ggplot
ggplot() + 
  geom_line(data = final_conc_set, aes(x = sample_collection_date, y = Concentration, color = collection_site)) + 
  geom_point(data = final_conc_set, aes(x = sample_collection_date, y = Concentration, shape = relative_lod, color = collection_site)) + 
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("above" = 16, "below" = 1)) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(x = "Sample Collection Date", 
       y = "Concentration", 
       shape = "Limit of Detection", 
       color = "Collection Site", 
       title = "One Month of Respiratory Disease Wastewater Testing") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1)) + 
  facet_wrap(.~Target)
Warning: Removed 60 rows containing missing values (`geom_line()`).
Warning: Removed 60 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).
# make ggplot
ggplot() + 
  geom_line(data = final_conc_set, aes(x = sample_collection_date, y = Concentration, color = collection_site)) + 
  geom_point(data = final_conc_set, aes(x = sample_collection_date, y = Concentration, shape = relative_lod, color = collection_site)) + 
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("above" = 16, "below" = 1)) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 6)) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(x = "Sample Collection Date", 
       y = "Concentration", 
       shape = "Limit of Detection", 
       color = "Collection Site", 
       title = "One Month of Respiratory Disease Wastewater Testing") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1)) + 
  facet_wrap(.~Target)
Warning: Removed 60 rows containing missing values (`geom_line()`).
Warning: Removed 60 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

Bonus comparison:

# final_conc_set vs. gc_calc_one_value

final_conc_set_slim <- final_conc_set %>% select(Sample, Target, relative_lod)

colnames(final_conc_set_slim) <- c("Sample", "Target", "conc_lod")

gc_calc_one_value_slim <- gc_calc_one_value %>% ungroup() %>% select(Sample, Target, relative_lod)

colnames(gc_calc_one_value_slim) <- c("Sample", "Target", "gc_lod")

all_lod_compare <- merge(final_conc_set_slim, gc_calc_one_value_slim, by = c("Sample", "Target"))

ggplot() + 
  geom_jitter(data = all_lod_compare, aes(x = conc_lod, y = gc_lod), alpha = 0.5, width = 0.3, height = 0.3) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(x = "Concentration LOD Calls", 
       y = "Gene Copies LOD Calls") + 
  facet_wrap(.~Target)

  • There are never instances where the Concentration LOD call is “below” and the Gene Copies LOD call is “above”

  • There are instances where the Concentration LOD call is “above” and the Gene Copies LOD call is “below”

The two above points imply that the Gene Copies LOD calls are more conservative than the Concentration LOD calls.
